identity.name: ITDR — Credential Stuffing (failed-login burst across many users)
identity.summary: The idp_signin_emulator emits a burst of FAILURE sign-ins from one source IP/ASN against many distinct user identifiers — the credential-stuffing shape where an attacker replays a stolen credential dump against an IdP. Cortex ITDR groups failed authentications by source IP, counts distinct targeted users in the window, and alerts when one origin sprays many accounts, with severity scaling as th…
identity.description: Credential stuffing is the highest-volume identity attack in the Verizon DBIR: an actor takes a breach-corpus of username/password pairs and replays them en masse against a login surface, accepting a low hit rate. The IdP-log signature is unmistakable — a single source IP (often one ASN / hosting provider) producing a wall of authentication FAILUREs across dozens of distinct usernames in a tight window, with the occasional SUCCESS where a reused password lands. This scenario drives CortexSim's idp_signin_emulator to POST a 20-identity failed-login burst from one source IP (Okta shape) and then a wider 50-identity burst (Microsoft Entra shape) into the identity collector. Detection is IdP-log analytics in Cortex ITDR: an XQL rule groups FAILURE authentications by source IP, counts distinct actor identifiers, and raises a credential-stuffing anomaly when the distinct-user count from one origin crosses threshold; severity escalates with the 50-identity burst. An NGFW EAL query corroborates the sustained outbound POST burst to the collector so the network plane confirms the volume. A correlation rule fuses the two-shape stuffing analytics into one identity-attack incident keyed on the source IP.

prerequisites.description: CortexSim idp_signin_emulator EAL plugin registered and exposing the credential_stuffing pattern.
Cortex ITDR ingesting okta_sso and msft_azure_ad_signin authentication datasets.

payload.code:
# Emit a 20-identity failed-login burst from one source IP (Okta), then a 50-identity burst (Microsoft).
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-003.yml --live
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-003-ms.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Many FAILURE authentications from one source IP across distinct user identifiers in a short window.
Sustained outbound HTTPS POST burst to the identity log collector.

biocs.name: ITDR-003 Pre-Flight idp_signin_emulator Metadata Visible
biocs.description: Pre-flight: idp_signin_emulator metadata visible to operator. Confirms the EAL plugin is registered and exposes the credential_stuffing pattern before the burst is emitted, so a null downstream result is a harness fault rather than a detection miss.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter eventType = "user.session.start" or _raw_log contains "credential_stuffing"
| comp count() as preflight_events by src_ip
| filter preflight_events >= 0

xql_queries.name: ITDR-003 Credential Stuffing Many Users One Source IP
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType = "user.session.start" and auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| comp count_distinct(actor_effective_username) as distinct_users, count() as fail_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, src_geo_country
| alter window_minutes = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 60000000)
| filter distinct_users >= 10 and window_minutes <= 30
| fields src_ip, src_geo_country, distinct_users, fail_count, window_minutes

xql_queries.name: ITDR-003 NGFW Sustained POST Burst To Identity Collector
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "ssl") and dst_port = 443
| filter uri_path contains "/idp/events" or url contains "/idp/events"
| comp count() as post_count, sum(bytes_sent) as bytes_out by src_ip, dst_host
| filter post_count >= 10
| fields src_ip, dst_host, post_count, bytes_out

xql_queries.name: ITDR-003 Credential Stuffing Severity Escalates 50 Event Burst
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| comp count_distinct(user_name) as distinct_users, count() as fail_count by src_ip
| alter severity = if(distinct_users >= 50, "critical", if(distinct_users >= 10, "high", "low"))
| filter distinct_users >= 50
| fields src_ip, distinct_users, fail_count, severity

correlation_rules.name: Credential Stuffing Identity Attack — Single Origin Spray
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(ITDR-003 Credential Stuffing Many Users One Source IP) OR XQL(ITDR-003 Credential Stuffing Severity Escalates 50 Event Burst)

analytics_modules: Cortex ITDR Analytics — Distinct-user failure spray per source IP
Cortex XSIAM Identity Analytics — Credential-stuffing origin detection

products.evidence_query: dataset = okta_sso | filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE" | comp count_distinct(actor_effective_username) as u by src_ip | filter u >= 10
Correlation CR-ITDR-0003 fuses the Okta and Microsoft Entra credential-stuffing analytics into one identity-attack incident keyed on source IP.

use_cases.name: Credential-Stuffing Detection
use_cases.description: Detect a single source IP attempting many distinct user identifiers in a short window across Okta and Microsoft Entra, with severity scaling to distinct-user volume.

test_cases.objective: A 20-identity failed-login burst from one IP raises a credential-stuffing anomaly.
preconditions: Cortex ITDR ingesting okta_sso and msft_azure_ad_signin datasets.
idp_signin_emulator authorized against the lab collector.
steps: Observe the credential-stuffing anomaly per source IP in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `ITDR-003 Credential Stuffing Many Users One Source IP` returns >= 1 row.
NGFW sustained-POST query corroborates the collector egress.
50-identity burst raises the escalated-severity query to >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Okta and Microsoft stuffing analytics stitch into one identity-attack incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-ITDR-0003` groups both shapes under one incident keyed on source IP.

preventive_controls: Enforce rate-limiting and CAPTCHA / bot-defense on the login surface per source IP and ASN.
Require MFA so a correct password alone never yields a session.
Subscribe to breach-credential feeds and force resets on exposed identities.
detection_engineering: Group authentication FAILUREs by source IP and alert when the distinct-user count crosses threshold in a window.
Scale severity to distinct-user volume so wide sprays escalate automatically.
Watch for the rare SUCCESS inside a failure spray — that account is now compromised.
